MuhammadYasir

From Pakistan into Motorsport. The only engineer worldwide selected for the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile (FIA) Motorsport Engineering Scholarship 2024-25. Six years of aerospace structural design and mission critical R&D at Pakistan's national aerospace engineering body. Cranfield MSc in Advanced Motorsport Engineering. Innovative problem solver, focused, systemic, and always eager to learn. Ready for the hardest problem you have. Core Values: Grace, Integrity, and Honesty.

01

The Story

I was born in Mardan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa - fourth child of a father who struggled to pay school fees but never once let that be an excuse. I grew up with one recurring complaint from every teacher: I ask too many questions. I never stopped.

In 2014 I topped the entire KPK province in pre-engineering - over 80,000 students - and was awarded a gold medal by the provincial minister. That earned a USAID scholarship to PIEAS, Pakistan's highest-ranked engineering university. I was the first in my family to attend university.

The day after completing my BS in 2018, I joined NDC (National Development Complex), responsible for Pakistan's aerospace and defence engineering. Led 10 plus engineers and technicians in the Design and Technology Development Group. Promoted to Manager in December 2023. Six years. Classified. Delivered.

Nothing has ever been enough. I resigned and won the FIA Motorsport Engineering Scholarship 2024 as sole global recipient at Cranfield University. Left my wife, my job, and my country to pursue the most demanding engineering discipline I could find. Worked on the most scientifically challenging and futuristic aspect of Motorsport: Integration of Liquid Hydrogen Internal Combustion Engine in Le Mans. My research sits at the frontier of Le Mans 2028.

Currently at Race and Rally, Northern Ireland: created and automated logistics and operations systems, resulting in GBP 100,000 plus monthly sales (a 52% rise within 06 months), zero errors.

Professional Experience

2025 - Present
Race and Rally, Northern Ireland, UK
Operations and Systems Engineer
Motorsport Supplychain - Technical Operations - Commercial Management - Digital Operations

Running the full commercial and operational backbone of a specialist global motorsport supply business - purchasing, logistics, inventory, quality, supplier relationships, customer technical support, and digital infrastructure. Managing GBP 100,000 plus in average monthly turnover across UK and European rally markets. Every system this business depends on was either built or rebuilt by me. Zero errors. Zero missed deadlines.

  • Designed and deployed fully automated purchasing and logistics workflows and streamlined systems, reducing fulfilment gaps to zero
  • Sole manager of all website and e-commerce operations - Shopify, Unleashed, Xero
  • Digital inventory and serialisation system for 4500 plus race-critical components - logging part numbers, life cycles, mileage, and service intervals
  • Pre and post-event spares reconciliation matrices aligned with parc ferme controls
  • Incoming inspection protocols for machined and composite parts - CMM-verified, reducing supplier non-conformances by 25%
  • Bills of Materials for race builds and upgrade kits across Rally 2, 3, and 4 platforms
  • Manage all logistics including international shipments, customs and taxation compliance, and delay management in a time-critical motorsport supply environment
  • Develop new product introduction strategies and operations - market analysis, supplier sourcing, pricing, and range integration for new component lines
  • Build stock performance dashboards, demand forecasting models, and inventory KPI frameworks to reduce overstock, eliminate critical stock-outs, and take purchasing decisions with live data
  • Manage the suppliers network - global B2B negotiations, new supplier acquisition, product range assessment, quality validation, and onboarding across high-performance motorsport components

2018 - 2024
NDC / NESCOM, Islamabad, Pakistan
Manager (Mechanical) - Design and Technology Development
National Development Complex - Aerospace and Defence - Promoted December 2023

Appointed the day after graduating. Led 10 plus engineers and technicians in the Design and Technology Development Group. Six years of mission-critical structural design and R&D across aerospace, defence, and heavy-industry applications.

  • Composite and hybrid aerospace structures - 20% mass reduction via FEA-driven stiffness optimisation
  • Designed and commissioned a 50 kt thermo-mechanical press for 3D aerospace composites - MIL-STD qualified, 22% cycle time improvement
  • FEA validation for cryogenic composite pressure vessels under ASME Section VIII - under 10% deviation from test
  • Structural and modal analysis for engine mounts and chassis - 12% mass reduction meeting full vibration criteria
  • Complete reverse engineering of Rotax engine via KREON 3D scanning - 10-micron tolerance
  • 10 plus MIL-STD qualification protocols developed - 25% improvement in defect detection
  • Power BI dashboards for NC root-cause prediction - 35% reduction in repeat non-conformances
  • DoE applied to composite filament winding - 18% improvement in material utilisation
  • Plant layout for honeycomb production facility - 28% reduction in handling time

05

Engineering Projects

05b

Research Theses

Two original research theses completed at PIEAS and Cranfield University. Click to view the full document.

MSc Thesis - Cranfield University 2025
CFD Simulation and Validation of Liquid Hydrogen Evaporation and Dispersion from Atomizer for Motorsport Safety Applications

ANSYS Fluent Eulerian multiphase simulation of LH2 jets under saturated and subcooled conditions for Le Mans 2028 safety systems. CFD results within 10% of experimental data. Supervisor: Dr Veronica Marchante Rodriguez. Award: 56%, 80 Credits.

View Full Thesis
BS Thesis - PIEAS 2018
Analysis and Optimisation of Gas Tungsten Arc Welding for High Strength Low Alloy Steel

Full Design of Experiments methodology across multiple sheet thicknesses and joint orientations. Variables: current, voltage, torch speed, shielding gas flow rate. Outputs: joint strength, HAZ width, residual strain. ANOVA statistical validation. PIEAS A+ Grade.

View Full Thesis
MSc Thesis Research Poster - Cranfield 2025
06

Le Mans Hypercar - Group Design Project

As part of MARLIN Motorsport at Cranfield University, we designed a complete Hydrogen ICE-based Le Mans Hypercar targeting the 2028 WEC regulations. I was responsible for the hydrogen storage, distribution, and safety systems.

Liquid Hydrogen Storage System with 260g/hr boil-off fully recovered into combustion. Cryogenic SCAM Pump - TRL 6 Self-Contained Actuating Magnetic Pump for LH2 distribution, developed with Barber-Nichols USA and the FIA. Custom vaporizer using the engine coolant loop - simultaneously warming fuel and cooling the engine. Pyrotechnic crash safety - full tank evacuation within 2.5 seconds on defined crash triggers.

Full Vehicle Specifications - MARLIN Motorsport
PowertrainPorsche 911 Turbo S 3.7L ICE - 520 kW
Front Axle300 kW Electric Motor Drive
StructureCarbon-Flax F1-Validated Crash Structure
AerodynamicsLow-Drag Package - Regressive Heave Spring
Predicted Lap Time3:29.446 - Le Mans Circuit
Fuel SystemLH2 - 260g/hr Boil-Off Recovery
SafetyPyrotechnic Vent - 2.5 Second Full Evacuation
GDP Score70% - 40 Credits

Collaboration: Barber-Nichols USA, Federation Internationale de l'Automobile. Supervisor: Cranfield Advanced Motorsport Engineering Staff.

FIA and Global Engagement

The only engineer in the world selected that year.

The FIA Motorsport Engineering Scholarship is awarded to one engineer globally per cycle. During the MSc I represented the Federation Internationale de l'Automobile globally, contributed research at the frontier of Le Mans 2028, and engaged with the highest levels of international motorsport governance.

Top: FIA Safety Week, Geneva 2025 - presenting to Global ASNs; with the FIA President. Bottom: British Grand Prix, Silverstone 2025 - FIA technical wing.

FIA Motorsport Engineering Scholarship 2024 to 2025Sole global recipient. Selected through multiple rounds of panel interviews with Cranfield and FIA directors.
FIA Safety Week - Geneva, 2025Invited delegate at FIA headquarters. Presented to Global ASNs on sustainability and hydrogen ICE safety. Engaged with the FIA President.
British Grand Prix - Silverstone, 2025Represented the FIA as support engineer with the technical wing. Engaged with Formula 1 team principals and senior international stakeholders.
